heading: Respite care
label: 1720B Respite care

Interim Extension of Respite Care Program, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–110, § 1(a)103 Stat. 682Oct. 6, 1989“Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection (c) of section 620B [now 1720B] of title 38, United States Code, the authority provided by such section shall terminate on .”November 30, 1989

Report, , , provided that if the Administrator of Veterans’ Affairs furnished respite care under this section, the Administrator was to conduct an evaluation of the health efficacy and cost-effectiveness of furnishing such care and submit to the Committees on Veterans’ Affairs of the Senate and House of Representatives not later than , a report containing the results of such evaluation and appropriate recommendations.Pub. children.label: a
children.text: The Secretary may furnish respite care services to a veteran who is enrolled to receive care under .section 1710 of this title
are furnished on an intermittent basis to a veteran who is suffering from a chronic illness and who resides primarily at home; and
are furnished for the purpose of helping the veteran to continue residing primarily at home.

children.label: c
children.text: In furnishing respite care services, the Secretary may enter into contract arrangements.
